mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects ongoing labor market expansion that supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day S&P 500 forward return is non-positive following months with positive month-over-month PAYEMS change over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service burdens, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices and an elevated federal funds rate generate a stagflationary impulse where input costs rise while policy restricts demand, jointly suppressing S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A regression of 20-day S&P 500 returns on the interaction between CPIAUCSL monthly change and FEDFUNDS level yields a positive coefficient or t-statistic magnitude below 1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in retail sales advances signals resilient consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes coincide with negative mean 5-day S&P 500 returns during periods when GDPC1 has expanded for 8 or more consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing activity and weakening corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months but S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment combined with decelerating nonfarm payroll growth signals labor market deterioration that simultaneously reduces household income and increases recession probability, jointly driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE rising more than 0.3pp over 3 months and month-over-month PAYEMS decline fails to produce negative mean 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in housing starts reflects collapsing residential investment and negative wealth effects that spill into equity risk sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 5% month-over-month are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth alongside accelerating consumer prices generates a negative supply shock that compresses corporate profit margins and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, jointly generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between quarterly GDPC1 growth rate and monthly CPIAUCSL change fails to exhibit a negative and statistically significant relationship with 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.
